subject: general-knowledge
description: Historic Places & Landmarks MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: The official residence of the Pope is in:
mcqs.explanation: The Pope resides in Vatican City, the smallest sovereign state in the world (0.44 km²), enclaved within Rome, Italy.

mcqs.q: The International Rice Research Institute (IRRI) is located in:
mcqs.explanation: IRRI is headquartered in Los Baños, Philippines.

mcqs.q: With which activity is Wall Street in New York, USA associated?
mcqs.explanation: Wall Street in lower Manhattan is the global hub of banking and finance (NYSE, Federal Reserve).

mcqs.q: Where is 'Élysée Palace'?
mcqs.explanation: The Élysée Palace in Paris is the official residence of the President of France.

mcqs.q: The famous 'Eiffel Tower' is in:
mcqs.explanation: The Eiffel Tower (1889) is in Paris, France — designed by Gustave Eiffel.

mcqs.q: 'Niagara Falls' is in:
mcqs.explanation: Niagara Falls is on the border between the USA (New York) and Canada (Ontario) — in North America.

mcqs.q: The Pentagon is:
options: A large building housing the USA Defence Ministry
mcqs.explanation: The Pentagon, in Arlington, Virginia, is the headquarters of the US Department of Defense.

mcqs.q: 'Golan Heights' belongs to:
mcqs.explanation: Golan Heights is internationally recognised as Syrian territory, though largely occupied by Israel since 1967.

mcqs.q: 'Waterloo' is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Waterloo is in Belgium — site of Napoleon's defeat in 1815.

mcqs.q: Where is the 'Leaning Tower of Pisa' situated?
mcqs.explanation: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is in Pisa, Italy.

mcqs.q: The 'Yellowstone National Park' is in:
mcqs.explanation: Yellowstone, the world's first national park (1872), is in the USA (mostly Wyoming).

mcqs.q: 'Tiananmen Square' is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Tiananmen Square is in central Beijing, China.

mcqs.q: 'Statue of Liberty' is situated in:
mcqs.explanation: The Statue of Liberty stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor.

mcqs.q: 'Bethlehem' is associated with:
mcqs.explanation: Bethlehem in Palestine is revered as the birthplace of Jesus Christ.

mcqs.q: 'Wimbledon', known for tennis, is in:
mcqs.explanation: Wimbledon, in southwest London, hosts The Championships — the oldest Grand Slam tennis tournament.

mcqs.q: What is the principal official residence of the British monarch in London?
mcqs.explanation: Buckingham Palace in London is the official London residence of the British monarch.

mcqs.q: What is the oldest and largest continuously inhabited castle in the world, used by the British monarch?
mcqs.explanation: Windsor Castle (~900 years old) is the oldest and largest continuously inhabited castle in the world.

mcqs.q: Which is the private country estate and winter retreat of the British royal family?
mcqs.explanation: Sandringham House in Norfolk is the British royal family's private country estate and winter retreat.

mcqs.q: Castle of Laeken in Brussels is the official residence of the King of which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Castle of Laeken in Brussels is the official residence of the King of the Belgians.

mcqs.q: The ceremonial residence of the King of Spain is:
mcqs.explanation: The Royal Palace of Madrid is the official/ceremonial residence of the King of Spain.

mcqs.q: Amalienborg Palace in Copenhagen is the official residence of the royal family of:
mcqs.explanation: Amalienborg Palace in Copenhagen is the official residence of the Danish royal family.

mcqs.q: Where is the official residence of the King of Sweden located?
mcqs.explanation: The Royal Palace of Stockholm is the official residence of the King of Sweden.

mcqs.q: The Royal Palace in Oslo is the official residence of the King of:
mcqs.explanation: The Royal Palace in Oslo is the official residence of the King of Norway.

mcqs.q: The Imperial Palace (Kōkyo) in Tokyo is the official residence of:
mcqs.explanation: The Imperial Palace (Kōkyo) in Tokyo is the residence of the Emperor of Japan.

mcqs.q: Istana Negara in Kuala Lumpur is the official residence of the King of:
mcqs.explanation: Istana Negara in Kuala Lumpur is the official residence of the King (Yang di-Pertuan Agong) of Malaysia.

mcqs.q: Istana Nurul Iman in Bandar Seri Begawan is the residence of:
mcqs.explanation: Istana Nurul Iman is the residence of the Sultan of Brunei — the world's largest residential palace.

mcqs.q: Basman Palace in Amman is the royal residence of the King of:
mcqs.explanation: Basman Palace in Amman is the royal residence of the King of Jordan.

mcqs.q: Al Alam Palace in Muscat is the ceremonial palace of the Sultan of:
mcqs.explanation: Al Alam Palace in Muscat is the ceremonial palace of the Sultan of Oman.

mcqs.q: Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh is the official residence and executive headquarters of the King of:
mcqs.explanation: Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh is the official residence and executive HQ of the King of Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: The primary workplace and official residence of the US President is:
mcqs.explanation: The White House at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington D.C. is the President's residence and primary workplace.

mcqs.q: The Grand Kremlin Palace in Moscow is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: The Grand Kremlin Palace in Moscow is the official residence of the President of Russia.

mcqs.q: The Élysée Palace in Paris is the official residence and office of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: The Élysée Palace in Paris has been the official residence of the President of France since 1848.

mcqs.q: Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi is the official residence of the President of India.

mcqs.q: Aiwan-e-Sadr in Islamabad is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Aiwan-e-Sadr in Islamabad is the official residence and workplace of the President of Pakistan.

mcqs.q: State House in Nairobi is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: State House in Nairobi is the official residence of the President of Kenya.

mcqs.q: Union Buildings in Pretoria is the seat and office complex of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Union Buildings in Pretoria is the seat of the South African government and office of the President.

mcqs.q: Akorda Presidential Palace in Astana is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: The Akorda Presidential Palace in Astana is the official residence of the President of Kazakhstan.

mcqs.q: Palácio da Alvorada in Brasília is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Palácio da Alvorada in Brasília is the official residence of the President of Brazil.

mcqs.q: Jubilee House in Accra is the official presidential residence of:
mcqs.explanation: Jubilee House in Accra is the official residence of the President of Ghana.

mcqs.q: Quirinal Palace in Rome is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Quirinal Palace in Rome has been the official residence of the President of Italy since 1946.

mcqs.q: Palácio de Belém in Lisbon is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Palácio de Belém in Lisbon is the official residence of the President of Portugal.

mcqs.q: President's House (Janadhipathi Mandiraya) in Colombo is the official residence of the President of:
mcqs.explanation: Janadhipathi Mandiraya (President's House) in Colombo is the official residence of the President of Sri Lanka.

mcqs.q: What is the official postal address of the White House?
mcqs.explanation: The White House is located at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington D.C.

mcqs.q: The main executive working office of the US President is known as:
mcqs.explanation: The Oval Office in the West Wing of the White House is the primary working office of the US President.

mcqs.q: Which location in Maryland (USA) serves as the President's official private retreat?
mcqs.explanation: Camp David in Maryland is the country retreat of the US President.

mcqs.q: What is the headquarters of the US Department of Defense in Arlington, Virginia?
mcqs.explanation: The Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia is the HQ of the US Department of Defense.

mcqs.q: 10 Downing Street in London is the official residence of the British:
mcqs.explanation: 10 Downing Street has been the official residence of the British Prime Minister since 1735.

mcqs.q: 7 Lok Kalyan Marg in New Delhi is the official residence of the Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: 7 Lok Kalyan Marg (formerly 7 Race Course Road) is the official residence of the Prime Minister of India.

mcqs.q: The Prime Minister's House in Islamabad is the official residence of the Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: The Prime Minister's House in Islamabad is the official residence of the PM of Pakistan.

mcqs.q: La Moncloa Palace in Madrid is the official residence of the Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: Palacio de la Moncloa in Madrid is the official residence of the PM of Spain.

mcqs.q: Temple Trees in Colombo is the official residence of the Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: Temple Trees is the official residence of the Prime Minister of Sri Lanka.

mcqs.q: Kantei in Tokyo is the official residence of the Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: Kantei (Sōri Daijin Kantei) in Tokyo is the official residence of the Prime Minister of Japan.

mcqs.q: Blair House in Washington D.C. serves as the official guest residence for:
mcqs.explanation: Blair House in Washington D.C. is the President's official guesthouse for visiting foreign heads of state.

mcqs.q: Akasaka Palace in Tokyo is Japan's state guest house for:
mcqs.explanation: Akasaka Palace is Japan's state guest house for official international delegations.

mcqs.q: El Pardo Palace in Madrid is the Spanish state guest residence for:
mcqs.explanation: El Pardo Palace in Madrid is the official guesthouse for visiting heads of state to Spain.

mcqs.q: The Palace of Nations in Geneva is the European headquarters of:
mcqs.explanation: The Palace of Nations (Palais des Nations) in Geneva is the European headquarters of the United Nations.

mcqs.q: Topkapi Palace in Istanbul is the former imperial residence of the:
mcqs.explanation: Topkapi Palace in Istanbul was the imperial residence of the Ottoman Sultans for about 400 years.

mcqs.q: The Blue House (Cheong Wa Dae) in Seoul was the former presidential residence of:
mcqs.explanation: The Blue House (Cheong Wa Dae) was the official residence of the President of South Korea (until 2022).

mcqs.q: The Space Needle is an iconic landmark located in which US city?
mcqs.explanation: The Space Needle (1962) is in Seattle, Washington.

mcqs.q: Which city in Italy is known as the Cradle of the Renaissance?
mcqs.explanation: Florence (Firenze) in Tuscany is known as the Cradle of the Renaissance.

mcqs.q: Venice, in Italy, is famously known as the:
mcqs.explanation: Venice is known as the City of Canals — built on 118 small islands in the Venetian Lagoon.

mcqs.q: Kirkuk, located in Iraq, is famous for its:
mcqs.explanation: Kirkuk in northern Iraq is famous for its rich oil fields.

mcqs.q: Machu Picchu, the ancient Incan city, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Machu Picchu, the 15th-century Inca citadel, is in the Andes of Peru.

mcqs.q: Moseley Road Baths are historic public baths located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Moseley Road Baths in Birmingham, UK are Grade II* listed Edwardian public baths.

mcqs.q: The Brandenburg Gate, a symbol of unity and freedom, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Brandenburg Gate (1791) is in Berlin — a symbol of German unity after the fall of the Berlin Wall.

mcqs.q: Canton is the historical name for which major commercial city in China?
mcqs.explanation: Canton is the historical Western name for Guangzhou, a major commercial port in southern China.

mcqs.q: Wentworth Woodhouse is a grand historic estate located in:
mcqs.explanation: Wentworth Woodhouse, with the longest country-house façade in Europe, is in South Yorkshire, England.

mcqs.q: Sharjah, known for its Islamic architecture and culture, is one of the seven emirates of:
mcqs.explanation: Sharjah is one of the 7 emirates of the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

mcqs.q: Bradlaugh Hall, a pre-partition political landmark, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Bradlaugh Hall (1900) in Lahore was a key venue for pre-partition political meetings.

mcqs.q: Gaza, a historic and contested city, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Gaza Strip is a Palestinian territory along the Mediterranean coast.

mcqs.q: Greenwich, part of London, is world-famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Greenwich is the location of the Royal Observatory — defining the Prime Meridian (0°) and Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).

mcqs.q: Greater Iqbal Park is located near Minar-e-Pakistan in which city?
mcqs.explanation: Greater Iqbal Park surrounds Minar-e-Pakistan in Lahore, Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Shukhov Tower is an engineering marvel located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Shukhov Tower (1922) in Moscow is a 160 m hyperboloid steel structure.

mcqs.q: FIFA House, the global headquarters of FIFA, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: FIFA House (the Home of FIFA) is in Zurich, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Vyborg is a historic city near the Finnish border located in:
mcqs.explanation: Vyborg is a Russian city in Leningrad Oblast, close to the Finnish border.

mcqs.q: Sagrada Família is a world-renowned basilica designed by Antoni Gaudí located in:
mcqs.explanation: Sagrada Família, designed by Antoni Gaudí, is in Barcelona, Spain.

mcqs.q: The Great Rift Valley stretches mainly through which region?
mcqs.explanation: The Great Rift Valley stretches ~7,000 km from Lebanon down through East Africa.

mcqs.q: Mount Eden Crater is a volcanic cone located in:
mcqs.explanation: Mount Eden is a volcanic crater in Auckland, New Zealand.

mcqs.q: Sudbury in Ontario, Canada, is famous for its:
mcqs.explanation: Sudbury, Ontario is the world's leading source of nickel.

mcqs.q: St Helena is famous as the island where which historical figure was exiled?
mcqs.explanation: Napoleon Bonaparte was exiled to St Helena, where he died in 1821.

mcqs.q: Gettysburg (USA) is known for a decisive battle in 1863 during the:
mcqs.explanation: The Battle of Gettysburg (1-3 July 1863) was a turning point of the American Civil War.

mcqs.q: Cape Canaveral (Florida, USA), formerly Cape Kennedy, is primarily known as a:
mcqs.explanation: Cape Canaveral is the principal launch site for NASA crewed and uncrewed missions.

mcqs.q: Epsom (England) is famous for what kind of event?
mcqs.explanation: Epsom Downs in Surrey, England hosts The Derby — the most prestigious flat horse race in the UK.

mcqs.q: Which city was the first targeted by an atomic bomb in 1945?
mcqs.explanation: Hiroshima was the first city ever targeted by an atomic bomb — 6 August 1945.

mcqs.q: Kew Gardens (Royal Botanic Gardens) are a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in:
mcqs.explanation: Kew Gardens (Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew) in London is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

mcqs.q: Lop Nur (China) served as the site of China's first:
mcqs.explanation: Lop Nur in Xinjiang was the site of China's first nuclear tests (from 1964).

mcqs.q: Bandung (Indonesia) hosted which historic event in 1955?
mcqs.explanation: The 1955 Bandung Conference brought together newly independent African and Asian nations — the foundation of the Non-Aligned Movement.

mcqs.q: Benghazi is a key port city on the Mediterranean coast of:
mcqs.explanation: Benghazi is Libya's second-largest city and a key Mediterranean port.

mcqs.q: Barcelona (Spain) is a chief port and cultural center that hosted the Olympic Games in:
mcqs.explanation: Barcelona hosted the 1992 Summer Olympic Games.

mcqs.q: Durban (South Africa) is historically linked with the political career of:
mcqs.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi began his political activism in Durban, South Africa (1893-1914).

mcqs.q: Kansas City (USA) is famous for:
mcqs.explanation: Kansas City is famous for livestock trade and BBQ culture.

mcqs.q: Aswan (Egypt) is noted for the Aswan High Dam and tourism on which river?
mcqs.explanation: Aswan in Egypt is noted for the Aswan High Dam (completed 1970) on the Nile River.

mcqs.q: Which city is called the 'Brasília of Pakistan' for its planned design?
mcqs.explanation: Islamabad — planned by Greek architect Constantinos Doxiadis — is called the 'Brasília of Pakistan'.

mcqs.q: Bethlehem (Palestine) is revered as the birthplace of:
mcqs.explanation: Bethlehem is revered as the birthplace of Jesus Christ.

mcqs.q: Auroville, founded by UNESCO in 1968 as an international township, is near which Indian city?
mcqs.explanation: Auroville, founded 1968 by Mirra Alfassa (The Mother), is near Pondicherry, India.

mcqs.q: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is one of the Seven Wonders of the World located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is in Pisa, Italy.

mcqs.q: Dar es Salaam is the largest city and commercial hub of:
mcqs.explanation: Dar es Salaam is Tanzania's largest city and main commercial port (Dodoma is the political capital).

mcqs.q: Bali is a world-famous island resort located in:
mcqs.explanation: Bali is an Indonesian island province famous for tourism and Hindu culture.

mcqs.q: The Catholic-Protestant conflict historically took place in Ulster, located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Troubles (Catholic-Protestant conflict) took place in Ulster — Northern Ireland.

mcqs.q: Wimbledon, famous for its Tennis Championships, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Wimbledon is in southwest London, UK.

mcqs.q: Baghdad (Iraq) lies on which river?
mcqs.explanation: Baghdad lies on the banks of the River Tigris.

mcqs.q: Jerusalem is a Holy City sacred to which religions?
mcqs.explanation: Jerusalem is holy to all three Abrahamic religions: Islam, Christianity and Judaism.

mcqs.q: Jerusalem was recaptured from the Crusaders in 1187 by:
mcqs.explanation: Salah al-Din Ayyubi (Saladin) recaptured Jerusalem from the Crusaders at the Battle of Hattin in 1187.

mcqs.q: Neelum Valley lies in which region?
mcqs.explanation: Neelum Valley lies in Azad Jammu & Kashmir, Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Which city in the West Bank is believed to be the world's oldest inhabited city?
mcqs.explanation: Jericho in the West Bank is widely regarded as the world's oldest continuously inhabited city (~9000 BCE).

mcqs.q: Waterloo in Belgium is where which military leader was defeated in 1815?
mcqs.explanation: Napoleon Bonaparte was defeated at the Battle of Waterloo (Belgium) on 18 June 1815.

mcqs.q: Timbuktu, a medieval center of Islamic learning, is in:
mcqs.explanation: Timbuktu, the legendary medieval centre of Islamic scholarship, is in Mali.

mcqs.q: Dasman Palace is located in which capital city?
mcqs.explanation: Dasman Palace is the official residence of the Emir of Kuwait in Kuwait City.

mcqs.q: The Sun Temple of Konark, dedicated to the Sun God, is in which Indian state?
mcqs.explanation: The 13th-century Sun Temple of Konark is in the Indian state of Odisha — a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

mcqs.q: Which city lies on two continents — Asia and Europe?
mcqs.explanation: Istanbul straddles the Bosphorus, with parts on both the Asian and European continents.

mcqs.q: Which is the smallest sovereign state in the world (0.44 sq km) and the residence of the Pope?
mcqs.explanation: Vatican City (0.44 km²) is the smallest sovereign state — also the residence of the Pope.

mcqs.q: Vatican City is enclaved within which major city?
mcqs.explanation: Vatican City is an independent city-state enclaved within Rome, Italy.

mcqs.q: Which ancient city in Jordan is known as the Rose-Red City?
mcqs.explanation: Petra in southern Jordan, carved into rose-red sandstone cliffs, is known as the Rose-Red City.

mcqs.q: Angkor Wat (Cambodia) holds what global distinction?
options: The largest religious monument in the world
mcqs.explanation: Angkor Wat in Cambodia is the world's largest religious monument.

mcqs.q: Which Japanese city is known as the City of Ten Thousand Shrines?
mcqs.explanation: Kyoto, the former imperial capital of Japan, is known as the City of Ten Thousand Shrines.

mcqs.q: Beijing (China) houses which famous UNESCO World Heritage site?
mcqs.explanation: The Forbidden City in central Beijing is a UNESCO World Heritage Site — the former imperial palace.

mcqs.q: Agra (India) is home to which New Seven Wonder of the World?
mcqs.explanation: The Taj Mahal in Agra is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.

mcqs.q: Which city is the holiest in Islam and home to the Kaaba?
mcqs.explanation: Mecca in Saudi Arabia is the holiest city in Islam and home to the Kaaba.

mcqs.q: Which city is known as the City of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Medina (al-Madina al-Munawwara) in Saudi Arabia is known as the City of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

mcqs.q: Dubai (UAE) is known for housing the world's tallest building, called the:
mcqs.explanation: The Burj Khalifa in Dubai (828 m) is the world's tallest building.

mcqs.q: Doha (Qatar) is known for modern architecture and hosting which major event in 2022?
mcqs.explanation: Doha (Qatar) hosted the FIFA World Cup in 2022.

mcqs.q: What is the capital and cultural heart of Iran?
mcqs.explanation: Tehran is the capital of Iran and its cultural and political heart.

mcqs.q: Athens (Greece) is often referred to as the:
mcqs.explanation: Athens is referred to as the Cradle of Democracy — birthplace of democratic governance.

mcqs.q: Rome (Italy) is known as the:
mcqs.explanation: Rome is known as the Eternal City (Roma Aeterna).

mcqs.q: Paris (France) is commonly known as the:
mcqs.explanation: Paris is known as the City of Light (La Ville Lumière).

mcqs.q: London (UK) is famous for landmarks like Buckingham Palace and:
mcqs.explanation: Big Ben (the Elizabeth Tower clock) is one of London's most iconic landmarks.

mcqs.q: Moscow (Russia) is known for Red Square and the:
mcqs.explanation: Moscow is famous for Red Square and the Kremlin (the seat of the Russian government).

mcqs.q: New York City (USA) is famously called the:
mcqs.explanation: New York City is famously called the Big Apple.

mcqs.q: Which US city is considered the political capital of the world?
mcqs.explanation: Washington D.C. is considered the political capital of the world — seat of US federal government.

mcqs.q: Rio de Janeiro (Brazil) is called the 'Marvelous City', which in Portuguese is:
mcqs.explanation: Rio de Janeiro's nickname 'Marvelous City' translates to 'Cidade Maravilhosa' in Portuguese.

mcqs.q: Sydney (Australia) is famous for its Harbour Bridge and:
mcqs.explanation: The Sydney Opera House is Sydney's most iconic landmark — alongside the Harbour Bridge.

mcqs.q: Which South African city lies beneath the iconic Table Mountain and is called the Mother City?
mcqs.explanation: Cape Town, beneath Table Mountain, is known as the Mother City (Moederstad).
